title: Bank of Japan raises interest rate to 1%, highest in 31 years
summary: The Bank of Japan raised its monetary policy rate to 1% to counter inflationary risks, leading commercial banks to increase deposit rates.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi participates in G7 summit in France
summary: Takaichi held bilateral meetings with the US president and the Italian prime minister, and proposed a framework for storing critical minerals.

title: Nikkei 225 index surpasses 70,000 points for the first time in history
summary: Japan's main stock index hit a record of 70,000 points, driven by hopes for peace between Iran and the United States and the Bank of Japan's rate hike.

title: Xi Jinping backs Myanmar's military government at summit in Beijing
summary: The Chinese president met with the Burmese leader and expressed support for the military regime, promoting its international reintegration.

title: Japan's FTC raids six ice cream manufacturers over alleged price cartel
summary: Competition authorities are investigating the producers for collusion to raise prices, causing discontent among Japanese consumers.

title: Japanese national soccer team advances in World Cup with strong performance
summary: Japan defeated the Netherlands in a friendly match with high viewership; Kamada and Nagatomo show good form as the team prepares for the tournament.

title: United States and Iran sign memorandum to end hostilities
summary: Both nations agreed to a cessation of hostilities and will open new negotiations on the humanitarian crisis and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Magnitude 5 earthquake shakes Gunma and Saitama prefectures, no tsunami warning
summary: An earthquake of intensity 5 on the Japanese scale struck southern Gunma and northern Saitama; the Tokai nuclear plant is checking for possible damage.

title: Japan approves basic plan to promote awareness of LGBTQ+ community
summary: After three years of development, the Japanese government approved a plan to foster understanding and prevent discrimination against LGBTQ+ people.

title: Japan moves toward passing a law penalizing damage to the national flag
summary: The bill, backed by four parties, criminalizes vandalizing the Japanese flag in order to protect the national symbol.

title: A 450-year-old sacred tree falls at Shimogamo Shrine in Kyoto
summary: The tree, considered a protective deity, collapsed in Tadasu-no-Mori forest without causing injuries; authorities are investigating the causes.

title: Municipal official arrested in Osaka over alleged bribery in public contracts
summary: An employee of Daito City Hall was arrested for allegedly receiving bribes to favor companies in the awarding of construction projects.

title: Iranian national soccer team debuts at World Cup amid protests and criticism
summary: Iranian players report having to leave the country after matches, while fans display protest flags during the first game.

title: Constitutional Democratic Party sanctions lawmaker for comments on Self-Defense Forces
summary: Lawmaker Koga was reprimanded for stating that members of the Self-Defense Forces come from low-income families, drawing cross-party criticism.

title: Eight dead in B-52 bomber crash in California
summary: A US Air Force B-52 bomber crashed during a test mission in California, killing eight people.

title: Ebola cases surpass 800 in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
summary: The WHO warns that the Ebola outbreak in the Congo could be larger than reported, with more than 800 confirmed cases and a limited humanitarian response.

title: Fujitsu president resigns over inappropriate conduct toward a woman
summary: Takahito Tokita resigned as president of Fujitsu after being accused of inappropriate behavior toward a female employee, sparking a corporate crisis.

title: Russians use VPNs and multiple devices to evade Putin's digital censorship
summary: Faced with tightening internet control in Russia, citizens are using virtual private networks and multiple phones to access free information.

title: Shohei Ohtani leads early voting for the MLB All-Star Game
summary: The Japanese star of the Angels is the most voted player in the first phase of All-Star Game voting, excelling as both a hitter and pitcher.

title: Shimane governor to undergo surgery for bladder cancer
summary: Governor Maruyama reported that he has stage 3 bladder cancer and will undergo surgery, though he will continue to perform his duties.
